observations: The Linear event is sufficient evidence to update the deal stage to won.
The won deal creates operational handoff work and a possible marketing opportunity, but not automatic external publication permission.
Laura should receive a routed recent-change opportunity package, not the full operational task surface.

state_proposals.interpretation: Southern Abrasives moved to won in Linear, creating both operational handoff needs and a possible marketing proof-point opportunity.

state_patch.summary: Southern Abrasives moved to won in Linear, creating an operational handoff and a possible marketing proof-point opportunity, but public naming and external announcement permission remain unresolved.
open_questions: Can Southern Abrasives be named publicly?
Which delivery or onboarding follow-up is required?
What proof point, if any, is safe to use in external marketing?

next_actions.summary: Patrick should confirm operational handoff, owner, next action, and any relationship-sensitive constraints.
Laura should review whether the won deal supports an internal proof-point note, customer story draft, LinkedIn draft, or no-op.
